_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-20 22:59:02Z)**_

## Audit Result: Largely Compliant — One Edge Case

### ✅ All Clear (7 endpoints)

Every `/api/master-data/*` endpoint writes to `Master_CV_Data.json` exclusively from the **Master CV tab** (Phase 8). All frontend calls originate from `populateMasterTab()` — no other workflow phase can reach them.

`publications.bib` is **never written** anywhere in the codebase.

---

### ⚠️ One Edge Case: `harvest_apply`

**`POST /api/harvest/apply`** (`web_app.py` ~line 5063) writes to `Master_CV_Data.json` but is triggered from the **Finalise tab** (Phase 7), not the Master CV management tab (Phase 8).

The harvest feature collects improved bullets, new skills, and summary variants from the completed CV and offers to write them back to the master file — a deliberate "apply what we learned" step.

**This isn't a bug so much as an intentional design question:** does harvest-apply count as part of the master file modification workflow, or is it a violation because it happens during Finalise?

Three options:

| Option | Approach |
|--------|----------|
| **A** | Move harvest-apply to only be callable from the Master CV tab — harvest produces candidates, user reviews/applies them in Phase 8 |
| **B** | Treat the Finalise → Harvest step as an extension of the master modification workflow and document it explicitly |
| **C** | Leave as-is and update the rule to explicitly permit this one Finalise-phase write |

How would you like to handle it?

_**Agent - sidechain (2026-03-20 22:59:47Z)**_

B.

### Overview

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-20 23:03:50Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>

```bash
gh issue create --repo Warnes-Innovations/cv-builder \
  --title "Feature: Edit publications.bib from the Master CV tab" \
  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

`publications.bib` is currently read-only in the application — it can be loaded and parsed, but there is no UI for adding, editing, or removing publication entries. Users must edit the raw BibTeX file manually outside the app.

## Problem

- Adding a new publication requires hand-editing `publications.bib` in a text editor and restarting/reloading
- No validation of BibTeX syntax is provided
- Typos in author names, years, or venue fields are not caught until CV generation
- The Master CV tab already supports editing experiences, skills, achievements, summaries, and education — publications is the only section with no UI

## Feature Request

Add publication editing to the **Master CV management tab**, consistent with the existing master-data editing pattern. Specifically:

- **List view** — show all publications in the current `publications.bib`, grouped or sortable by year/type
- **Add** — form for entering a new BibTeX entry (key fields: cite key, type, title, authors, year, venue/journal, DOI)
- **Edit** — inline editing of individual fields on an existing entry
- **Delete** — remove an entry with confirmation
- **BibTeX import** — paste raw BibTeX text to import one or more entries at once
- **Validation** — flag missing required fields (title, authors, year) on save

## Implementation Notes

- Backend: new `/api/master-data/publication` endpoint following the same pattern as `/api/master-data/achievement`, `/api/master-data/skill`, etc.
- `publications.bib` must only be written from this Master CV management stage — never during customization or generation (see project CRITICAL rule in `.github/copilot-instructions.md`)
- Parser: `scripts/utils/bibtex_parser.py` already handles read; the write path needs a serializer
- The existing publications review table (customization phase) is read-only and should remain so
EOF
)"
```

```text
https://github.com/Warnes-Innovations/cv-builder/issues/12
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-20 23:06:25Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>

```bash
gh issue comment 12 --repo Warnes-Innovations/cv-builder --body "## Amendment: LLM-assisted publication entry

In addition to structured form entry and raw BibTeX paste, the add-publication UI should support **unstructured text input**:

- **Free-text area** — user types or pastes a publication in any format (plain text citation, DOI URL, PubMed abstract, copy-paste from a CV PDF, etc.)
- **File upload** — user uploads a plain text file containing one or more publication references in any format

In both cases the input is sent to the LLM, which converts it to valid BibTeX entries. The returned BibTeX is shown to the user for review/correction before being saved to \`publications.bib\`.

This removes the need for users to know BibTeX syntax for the common case of adding publications they already have in some other format."
```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-20 23:32:13Z)**_

Good question to resolve before planning. Here's the tradeoff:

**Worktree (from `cv-builder`)**
- Keeps `cv-builder-prod` pointed at `main` for reference/comparison during development
- Isolated — changes don't affect other open files you may have from prod
- Agent tool supports `isolation: "worktree"` natively, which auto-creates and cleans up
- Slightly more setup overhead

**Directly in `cv-builder-prod`**
- Simpler — just `git checkout -b feature/...` and work there
- You lose easy access to the `main` state in that directory while on the branch
- No cleanup needed when done

Given Issue #9 is a **substantial feature** (new LLM pass, new UI components, new rendering logic) that will take multiple iterations, I'd recommend a **worktree** — it lets you keep `cv-builder-prod` on `main` so you can always compare against production behavior without switching branches.

Which do you prefer?

```text
     3→
     4→Loads configuration from multiple sources in priority order:

     6→2. .env file (if present)
     7→3. config.yaml (default settings)
     8→4. Hardcoded defaults (fallback)
     9→"""
    10→
    11→import os
    12→import yaml
    13→from pathlib import Path
    14→from typing import Any, Dict, Optional
    15→from dotenv import load_dotenv

    18→class Config:
    19→    """Configuration manager for CV Builder."""
    20→    
    21→    def __init__(self, config_file: Optional[str] = None, load_env: bool = True):
    22→        """
    23→        Initialize configuration.
    24→        
    25→        Args:
    26→            config_file: Path to config.yaml (default: ./config.yaml)
    27→            load_env: Whether to load .env file (default: True)
    28→        """
    29→        self._config: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    30→        
    31→        # Load .env file if requested
    32→        if load_env:
    33→            env_file = Path.cwd() / ".env"
    34→            if env_file.exists():
    35→                load_dotenv(env_file)
    36→        
    37→        # Load config.yaml
    38→        if config_file is None:
    39→            config_file = Path.cwd() / "config.yaml"
    40→        else:
    41→            config_file = Path(config_file)
    42→        
    43→        if config_file.exists():
    44→            with open(config_file, 'r') as f:
    45→                self._config = yaml.safe_load(f) or {}
    46→        
    47→        # Expand home directory paths

    50→    def _expand_paths(self):
    51→        """Expand ~ in paths to absolute paths."""
    52→        if 'data' in self._config:
    53→            for key in ['master_cv', 'publications', 'output_dir']:
    54→                if key in self._config['data']:
    55→                    path = self._config['data'][key]
    56→                    if isinstance(path, str) and path.startswith('~'):
    57→                        self._config['data'][key] = str(Path(path).expanduser())
    58→        
    59→        if 'session' in self._config:
    60→            for key in ['session_dir', 'history_file']:
    61→                if key in self._config['session']:
    62→                    path = self._config['session'][key]
    63→                    if isinstance(path, str) and path.startswith('~'):

    80→    def get(self, key: str, default: Any = None) -> Any:
    81→        """Get configuration value with dot notation support."""
    82→        keys = key.split('.')
    83→        value = self._config
    84→        
    85→        for k in keys:

    88→                if value is None:
    89→                    return default

    91→                return default
    92→        
    93→        return value

   254→
   255→class ConfigurationError(Exception):
   256→    """Raised when the configuration is invalid or missing required values."""
   257→
   258→
   259→# Global config instance
   260→_config: Optional[Config] = None
   261→
   262→
   263→def get_config(reload: bool = False) -> Config:
   264→    """
   265→    Get global configuration instance.
   266→
   267→    Args:
   268→        reload: Force reload configuration
   269→
   270→    Returns:
   271→        Config instance
   272→    """
   273→    global _config
   274→    if _config is None or reload:
   275→        _config = Config()
   276→    return _config
   277→
   278→
   279→def validate_config(provider: Optional[str] = None) -> None:
   280→    """Validate that required configuration values are present.
   281→
   282→    Call at application startup (before first request).  Pass the resolved
   283→    provider string (from CLI arg or env override) so that explicit CLI
   284→    values are accepted even when config.yaml is sparse.
   285→
   286→    Raises:
   287→        ConfigurationError: if no LLM provider is configured from any source.
   288→    """
   289→    effective_provider = provider or get_config().llm_provider
   290→    if not effective_provider or not str(effective_provider).strip():
   291→        raise ConfigurationError(
   292→            "No LLM provider configured. "
   293→            "Set `llm.default_provider` in config.yaml or pass `--llm-provider` "
   294→            "on the command line. "
   295→            "Valid values: copilot-oauth, copilot, github, openai, anthropic, gemini, groq, local, copilot-sdk."
   296→        )
   297→
```

```text
     1→"""
     2→CV Orchestrator - Bridges LLM intelligence with document generation utilities.
     3→
     4→This module coordinates between:
     5→- LLM-driven content selection
     6→- Traditional utility functions (scoring, formatting)
     7→- Document generation (DOCX/PDF)
     8→"""
     9→
    10→import copy
    11→import json
    12→import re
    13→import sys
    14→import time
    15→from pathlib import Path
    16→from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Any
    17→from datetime import datetime
    18→import subprocess
    19→import weasyprint
    20→from collections import defaultdict
    21→
    22→# Import existing utilities
    23→from .scoring import (
    24→    calculate_relevance_score,
    25→    calculate_skill_score
    26→)
    27→from .bibtex_parser import parse_bibtex_file, format_publication
    28→from .config import get_config
    29→from .llm_client import LLMClient
    30→
    31→
    32→class CVOrchestrator:
    33→    """Orchestrates CV generation with LLM + utilities."""
    34→    
    35→    def __init__(
    36→        self,
    37→        master_data_path: str,
    38→        publications_path: str,
    39→        output_dir: str,
    40→        llm_client: LLMClient
    41→    ):
    42→        self.master_data_path = Path(master_data_path).expanduser()
    43→        self.publications_path = Path(publications_path).expanduser()
    44→        self.output_dir = Path(output_dir).expanduser()
    45→        self.llm = llm_client
    46→        
    47→        # Load master data
    48→        self.master_data = self._load_master_data()
    49→
    50→        # Load publications if available
    51→        self.publications = {}
    52→        if self.publications_path.exists():
    53→            self.publications = parse_bibtex_file(str(self.publications_path))
    54→
    55→        # Load synonym map for ATS skill normalisation
    56→        self._synonym_map: Dict[str, str] = self._load_synonym_map()
    57→        # Full expansion index: any form (lower) -> canonical
    58→        self._expansion_index: Dict[str, str] = {}
    59→        for alias, canonical in self._synonym_map.items():
    60→            if alias.startswith('_'):  # skip comment keys
    61→                continue
    62→            self._expansion_index[alias.lower()] = canonical
    63→            self._expansion_index[canonical.lower()] = canonical
    64→    
    65→    def _load_master_data(self) -> Dict:
    66→        """Load Master_CV_Data.json."""
    67→        if not self.master_data_path.exists():
    68→            raise FileNotFoundError(
    69→                f"Master data file not found: {self.master_data_path}\n"
    70→                "Please create Master_CV_Data.json first."
    71→            )
    72→        
    73→        with open(self.master_data_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    74→            return json.load(f)
    75→
    76→    def _load_synonym_map(self) -> Dict[str, str]:
    77→        """Load scripts/data/synonym_map.json, returning {} gracefully if missing."""
    78→        map_path = Path(__file__).parent.parent / 'data' / 'synonym_map.json'
    79→        if not map_path.exists():
    80→            return {}
    81→        try:
    82→            with open(map_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    83→                data = json.load(f)
    84→            return {k: v for k, v in data.items() if not k.startswith('_')}
    85→        except Exception:
    86→            return {}
    87→
    88→    def canonical_skill_name(self, name: str) -> str:
    89→        """Return the canonical form of a skill name using the synonym map.
    90→
    91→        Examples: 'ML' -> 'Machine Learning', 'sklearn' -> 'scikit-learn'.
    92→        Unknown names are returned unchanged.
    93→        """
    94→        return self._expansion_index.get(name.lower(), name)
    95→
    96→    def _prepare_cv_data_for_template(
    97→        self,
    98→        selected_content: Dict,
    99→        job_analysis: Dict,
   100→        template_variant: str = 'standard'
   101→    ) -> Dict:
   102→        """Prepare CV data in the format expected by the HTML resume template."""
   103→
   104→        # Work on copies so template-specific normalization never mutates the
   105→        # session's selected content in place.
   106→        personal_info = copy.deepcopy(selected_content.get('personal_info', {}))

   108→        # Validate contact information

   121→                personal_info.get('languages', [])
   122→            )
   123→
   124→        # Get professional summary

   129→        # Format skills by category

   131→            selected_content.get('skills', []), 
   132→            template_variant
   133→        )
   134→        
   135→        # Format publications
   136→        publications = self._format_publications(selected_content.get('publications', []))
   137→
   138→        experiences = self._normalize_experiences_for_template(
   139→            copy.deepcopy(selected_content.get('experiences', []))
   140→        )
   141→        achievements = self._normalize_achievement_entries(
   142→            copy.deepcopy(selected_content.get('achievements', []))
   143→        )
   144→
   145→        # Get awards and certifications
   146→        awards = selected_content.get('awards', [])
   147→        certifications = selected_content.get('certifications', [])
   148→        
   149→        # Add template metadata
   150→        template_metadata = {
   151→            'variant': template_variant,
   152→            'generated_date': datetime.now().isoformat(),
   153→            'job_title': job_analysis.get('title', ''),
   154→            'company': job_analysis.get('company', ''),
   155→            'total_publications_count': len(self.publications) if self.publications else 0,
   156→        }
   157→        
   158→        cv_data = {
   159→            'personal_info': personal_info,
   160→            'professional_summary': professional_summary,
   161→            'experiences': experiences,
   162→            'achievements': achievements,
   163→            'education': selected_content.get('education', []),
   164→            'skills_by_category': skills_by_category,
   165→            'awards': awards,
   166→            'certifications': certifications,
   167→            'publications': publications,
   168→            'template_metadata': template_metadata
   169→        }
   170→
   171→        return cv_data
   172→
   173→    @staticmethod
   174→    def _extract_display_text(item: Any, preferred_fields: Optional[List[str]] = None) -> str:
   175→        """Extract the best human-readable text from a template item."""
   176→        if item is None:
   177→            return ''
   178→        if isinstance(item, str):
   179→            return item.strip()
   180→        if not isinstance(item, dict):
   181→            return str(item).strip()
   182→
   183→        field_order = preferred_fields or []
   184→        fallback_fields = [
   185→            'text',
   186→            'description',
   187→            'summary',
   188→            'formatted',
   189→            'formatted_citation',
   190→            'title',
   191→            'name',
   192→            'degree',
   193→            'institution',
   194→            'language',
   195→            'value',
   196→        ]
   197→
   198→        for field_name in field_order + fallback_fields:
   199→            value = item.get(field_name)
   200→            if isinstance(value, str) and value.strip():
   201→                return value.strip()
   202→
   203→        return ''
   204→
   205→    def _normalize_achievement_entries(self, achievements: List[Any]) -> List[Any]:
   206→        """Ensure achievement-like entries always expose a human-readable ``text``."""
   207→        normalized = []
   208→        for achievement in achievements or []:
   209→            if isinstance(achievement, dict):
   210→                entry = dict(achievement)
   211→                entry['text'] = self._extract_display_text(
   212→                    entry,
   213→                    preferred_fields=['text', 'description', 'summary', 'title', 'name'],
   214→                )
   215→                normalized.append(entry)
   216→            else:
   217→                normalized.append(achievement)
   218→        return normalized
   219→
   220→    def _normalize_experiences_for_template(self, experiences: List[Any]) -> List[Any]:
   221→        """Normalize experience achievement payloads before template rendering."""
   222→        normalized = []
   223→        for experience in experiences or []:
   224→            if not isinstance(experience, dict):
   225→                normalized.append(experience)
   226→                continue
   227→
   228→            entry = dict(experience)
   229→            for key in ('ordered_achievements', 'achievements'):
   230→                if isinstance(entry.get(key), list):
   231→                    entry[key] = self._normalize_achievement_entries(entry.get(key, []))
   232→            normalized.append(entry)
   233→        return normalized
   234→
   235→    def _normalize_language_entries(self, languages: List[Any]) -> List[str]:
   236→        """Convert language records to simple display strings for the template."""
   237→        normalized = []
   238→        for language in languages or []:
   239→            if isinstance(language, dict):
   240→                name = self._extract_display_text(
   241→                    language,
   242→                    preferred_fields=['language', 'name'],
   243→                )
   244→                proficiency = str(language.get('proficiency', '')).strip()
   245→                if name and proficiency:
   246→                    normalized.append(f"{name} ({proficiency})")
   247→                elif name:
   248→                    normalized.append(name)
   249→            else:
   250→                text = str(language).strip()
   251→                if text:
   252→                    normalized.append(text)
   253→        return normalized
   254→
   255→    def _organize_skills_by_category(self, skills: List[Dict], variant: str) -> List[Dict]:
   256→        """Organize skills by category, deduplicating by canonical synonym name."""
   257→        if not skills:
   258→            return []
   259→
   260→        # Deduplicate within the full list by canonical name.
   261→        # If 'ML' and 'Machine Learning' both appear, merge them: keep the one
   262→        # with more years and collect aliases from the other.
   263→        canonical_seen: Dict[str, Dict] = {}  # canonical_lower -> merged skill dict
   264→        for skill in skills:
   265→            name = skill.get('name', '')
   266→            canonical = self.canonical_skill_name(name)
   267→            key = canonical.lower()
   268→            if key not in canonical_seen:
   269→                merged = dict(skill)
   270→                merged['name'] = canonical if canonical != name else name
   271→                merged.setdefault('aliases', list(skill.get('aliases') or []))
   272→                if canonical != name and name not in merged['aliases']:
   273→                    merged['aliases'].append(name)
   274→                canonical_seen[key] = merged
   275→            else:
   276→                existing = canonical_seen[key]
   277→                # Keep the entry with more years; add the other name as alias
   278→                if skill.get('years', 0) > existing.get('years', 0):
   279→                    alias_name = existing.get('name', '')
   280→                    existing.update({k: v for k, v in skill.items() if k != 'aliases'})
   281→                    existing['name'] = canonical
   282→                    existing.setdefault('aliases', [])
   283→                    if alias_name and alias_name not in existing['aliases']:
   284→                        existing['aliases'].append(alias_name)
   285→                else:
   286→                    existing.setdefault('aliases', [])
   287→                    if name and name not in existing['aliases'] and name != existing['name']:
   288→                        existing['aliases'].append(name)
   289→
   290→        deduped_skills = list(canonical_seen.values())
   291→
   292→        category_skills: Dict[str, List[Dict]] = defaultdict(list)
   293→        for skill in deduped_skills:
   294→            category = skill.get('category', 'General')
   295→            category_skills[category].append(skill)
   296→
   297→        # Define category priority
   298→        priority_orders = {
   299→            'standard': ['Core Expertise', 'Programming', 'Technical', 'Tools', 'General'],
   300→            'technical': ['Programming', 'Technical', 'Tools', 'Core Expertise', 'General'],
```

```text
  1627→    @staticmethod
  1628→    def normalize_skills_data(skills_data) -> List[str]:
  1629→        """Normalize skills data to a canonical flat list format.
  1630→
  1631→        Accepts multiple schema formats:
  1632→        - Flat list: []
  1633→        - Dict of categories with 'skills' key: {cat_name: {skills: [...]}}
  1634→        - Dict of lists (legacy): {cat_name: [...]}
  1635→
  1636→        Returns a flat list [skills...] as the canonical format.
  1637→
  1638→        Parameters
  1639→        ----------
  1640→        skills_data
  1641→            Raw skills data in any of the supported formats
  1642→
  1643→        Returns
  1644→        -------
  1645→        List[str]
  1646→            Normalized flat list of skills
  1647→        """
  1648→        if not skills_data:
  1649→            return []
  1650→
  1651→        # Already a flat list
  1652→        if isinstance(skills_data, list):
  1653→            return skills_data
  1654→
  1655→        # Dict of categories
  1656→        if isinstance(skills_data, dict):
  1657→            all_skills = []
  1658→            for category_data in skills_data.values():
  1659→                # Category with nested 'skills' key
  1660→                if isinstance(category_data, dict) and 'skills' in category_data:
  1661→                    category_skills = category_data.get('skills', [])
  1662→                    if isinstance(category_skills, list):
  1663→                        all_skills.extend(category_skills)
  1664→                # Legacy: category_data is directly a list
  1665→                elif isinstance(category_data, list):
  1666→                    all_skills.extend(category_data)
  1667→            return all_skills
  1668→
  1669→        return []
  1670→
```

```text
   202→    def recommend_customizations(
   203→        self,
   204→        job_analysis: Dict,
   205→        master_data: Dict,
   206→        user_preferences: Dict = None,
   207→        conversation_history: List[Dict] = None,
   208→    ) -> Dict:
   209→        """Recommend CV customisations for a specific job using the LLM.
   210→
   211→        The prompt is provider-agnostic; subclasses only need to implement chat().
   212→        """
   213→        # ── Extract only the fields the prompt actually needs ─────────────────
   214→        job_summary = {
   215→            k: job_analysis.get(k)
   216→            for k in (
   217→                'title', 'company', 'domain', 'role_level',
   218→                'required_skills', 'preferred_skills',
   219→                'must_have_requirements', 'nice_to_have_requirements',
   220→                'ats_keywords', 'culture_indicators',
   221→            )
   222→            if job_analysis.get(k)
   223→        }
   224→
   225→        # ── User preferences block ─────────────────────────────────────────────
   226→        prefs_block = ""
   227→        if user_preferences:
   228→            lines = ["=" * 72,
   229→                     "CRITICAL USER INSTRUCTIONS — OVERRIDE ALL OTHER CONSIDERATIONS:",
   230→                     "=" * 72]
   231→            for pref_type, pref_value in user_preferences.items():
   232→                lines.append(f"\n{pref_type.upper()}:\n{pref_value}")
   233→            lines += [
   234→                "\n" + "=" * 72,
   235→                "COMPLIANCE REQUIRED:",
   236→                "- 'omit'/'exclude' a named company/experience → set its recommendation to Omit",
   237→                "- 'focus on'/'emphasize' a type of work → set those entries to Emphasize",
   238→                "- named achievements/projects to highlight → set related entries to Emphasize or Include",
   239→                "=" * 72 + "\n",
   240→            ]
   241→            prefs_block = "\n".join(lines) + "\n\n"
   242→
   243→        # ── Conversation history block ─────────────────────────────────────────
   244→        history_block = ""
   245→        if conversation_history:
   246→            lines = ["RECENT CONVERSATION (additional user preferences may be here):",
   247→                     "-" * 60]
   248→            for msg in conversation_history:
   249→                role = msg.get('role', 'unknown').capitalize()
   250→                text = msg.get('content', '')[:600]
   251→                lines.append(f"{role}: {text}")
   252→            lines.append("-" * 60 + "\n")
   253→            history_block = "\n".join(lines) + "\n"
   254→
   255→        # ── Experience and achievement lists ──────────────────────────────────
   256→        exp_lines = "\n".join(
   257→            f"- {exp.get('id', '')}: {exp.get('title', '')} at {exp.get('company', '')}"
   258→            for exp in master_data.get('experience', [])
   259→        )
   260→        ach_lines = "\n".join(
   261→            f"- {ach.get('id', '')}: {ach.get('title', '')} "
   262→            f"(relevant for: {', '.join(ach.get('relevant_for', []))})"
   263→            for ach in master_data.get('selected_achievements', [])
   264→        )
   265→
   266→        n_exp = len(master_data.get('experience', []))
   267→        n_ach = len(master_data.get('selected_achievements', []))
   268→
   269→        prompt = f"""{prefs_block}{history_block}Job Analysis:
   270→{json.dumps(job_summary, indent=2)}
   271→
   272→Available Experiences ({n_exp} total):
   273→{exp_lines or '(none)'}
   274→
   275→Available Key Achievements ({n_ach} total):
   276→{ach_lines or '(none)'}
   277→
   278→STEP 1 — Extract constraints from user instructions above (if any):
   279→- List any companies/experiences to OMIT
   280→- List any work types/skills to EMPHASIZE
   281→- List any achievements to HIGHLIGHT
   282→
   283→STEP 2 — For EACH experience, provide THREE independent assessments:
   284→
   285→1. RECOMMENDATION (job relevance):
   286→   "Emphasize"    — highly relevant, feature prominently
   287→   "Include"      — relevant, standard treatment
   288→   "De-emphasize" — marginally relevant, brief mention
   289→   "Omit"         — not relevant, exclude
   290→
   291→2. CONFIDENCE (how certain you are of the recommendation, not relevance):
   292→   "Very High" / "High" / "Medium" / "Low" / "Very Low"
   293→   Example: "Emphasize" + "Medium" = very relevant but CV evidence is sparse.
   294→
   295→3. REASONING — 2-3 sentences: why this recommendation, what evidence, any assumptions.
   296→
   297→STEP 3 — Same three-part structure for Key Achievements.
   298→
   299→STEP 4 — Suggest NEW achievements not already in the list above.
   300→For each experience where you can infer a strong, evidence-backed accomplishment
   301→from the job description, industry context, or role level that is NOT already
   302→listed, propose it as a suggested achievement. Only suggest achievements that:
   303→- Are credible given the experience's title, company, and time period
   304→- Are directly relevant to this specific job posting
   305→- Are NOT already captured in the Available Key Achievements list above
   306→Limit to at most 2 suggestions per experience; omit if none are credible.
   307→
   308→For skills: only flag skills that are notably relevant (Emphasize/Include) or
   309→notably irrelevant/misleading (De-emphasize/Omit) — skip unremarkable ones.
   310→
   311→Return ONLY a JSON object — no prose, no markdown fences:
   312→{{
```

```text
    37→def select_content(

   100→            )
```

```text
   255→    def _organize_skills_by_category(self, skills: List[Dict], variant: str) -> List[Dict]:
   256→        """Organize skills by category, deduplicating by canonical synonym name."""
   257→        if not skills:
   258→            return []
   259→
   260→        # Deduplicate within the full list by canonical name.
   261→        # If 'ML' and 'Machine Learning' both appear, merge them: keep the one
   262→        # with more years and collect aliases from the other.
   263→        canonical_seen: Dict[str, Dict] = {}  # canonical_lower -> merged skill dict
   264→        for skill in skills:
   265→            name = skill.get('name', '')
   266→            canonical = self.canonical_skill_name(name)
   267→            key = canonical.lower()
   268→            if key not in canonical_seen:
   269→                merged = dict(skill)
   270→                merged['name'] = canonical if canonical != name else name
   271→                merged.setdefault('aliases', list(skill.get('aliases') or []))
   272→                if canonical != name and name not in merged['aliases']:
   273→                    merged['aliases'].append(name)
   274→                canonical_seen[key] = merged
   275→            else:
   276→                existing = canonical_seen[key]
   277→                # Keep the entry with more years; add the other name as alias
   278→                if skill.get('years', 0) > existing.get('years', 0):
   279→                    alias_name = existing.get('name', '')
   280→                    existing.update({k: v for k, v in skill.items() if k != 'aliases'})
   281→                    existing['name'] = canonical
   282→                    existing.setdefault('aliases', [])
   283→                    if alias_name and alias_name not in existing['aliases']:
   284→                        existing['aliases'].append(alias_name)
   285→                else:
   286→                    existing.setdefault('aliases', [])
   287→                    if name and name not in existing['aliases'] and name != existing['name']:
   288→                        existing['aliases'].append(name)
   289→
   290→        deduped_skills = list(canonical_seen.values())
   291→
   292→        category_skills: Dict[str, List[Dict]] = defaultdict(list)
   293→        for skill in deduped_skills:
   294→            category = skill.get('category', 'General')
   295→            category_skills[category].append(skill)
   296→
   297→        # Define category priority
   298→        priority_orders = {
   299→            'standard': ['Core Expertise', 'Programming', 'Technical', 'Tools', 'General'],
   300→            'technical': ['Programming', 'Technical', 'Tools', 'Core Expertise', 'General'],
   301→            'academic': ['Research', 'Technical', 'Programming', 'Core Expertise', 'General']
   302→        }
   303→
   304→        priority_order = priority_orders.get(variant, priority_orders['standard'])
   305→
   306→        sorted_categories = []
   307→
   308→        # Add priority categories first
   309→        for category in priority_order:
   310→            if category in category_skills:
   311→                skills_list = sorted(category_skills[category],
   312→                                     key=lambda x: (-x.get('years', 0), x.get('name', '')))
   313→                sorted_categories.append({
   314→                    'category': category,
   315→                    'skills': skills_list
   316→                })
   317→
   318→        # Add remaining categories alphabetically
   319→        remaining_categories = sorted(set(category_skills.keys()) - set(priority_order))
   320→        for category in remaining_categories:
   321→            skills_list = sorted(category_skills[category],
   322→                                 key=lambda x: (-x.get('years', 0), x.get('name', '')))
   323→            sorted_categories.append({
   324→                'category': category,
   325→                'skills': skills_list
   326→            })
   327→
   328→        return sorted_categories
   329→    
   330→    def _format_publications(self, publications: List) -> List[Dict]:
   331→        """Format publications for template consumption."""
   332→        owner_name = self.master_data.get('personal_info', {}).get('name', '') if self.master_data else ''
   333→        # Extract last name: handle "Last, First" (BibTeX/comma style) and "First Last" (natural)
   334→        if ',' in owner_name:
   335→            owner_last = owner_name.split(',')[0].strip().lower()
   336→        else:
   337→            owner_last = owner_name.strip().split()[-1].lower() if owner_name.strip() else ''
   338→
   339→        formatted_pubs = []
   340→        for pub in publications:
   341→            if isinstance(pub, dict):
   342→                entry: Dict[str, Any] = {}
   343→                if 'formatted' in pub:
   344→                    entry['formatted_citation'] = pub['formatted']
   345→                elif 'title' in pub:
   346→                    authors = pub.get('authors', 'Unknown')
   347→                    title = pub.get('title', '')
   348→                    journal = pub.get('journal', '')
   349→                    year = pub.get('year', '')
   350→                    citation = f"{authors}. {title}. {journal} ({year}).".strip()
   351→                    entry['formatted_citation'] = citation
   352→                else:
   353→                    continue
   354→
   355→                # Detect first authorship: compare owner last name against leading author token
   356→                if owner_last:
   357→                    raw_authors = pub.get('authors', '')
   358→                    first_token = raw_authors.split(',')[0].strip().lower() if raw_authors else ''
   359→                    entry['is_first_author'] = bool(first_token and owner_last in first_token)
   360→                else:
   361→                    entry['is_first_author'] = False
   362→
   363→                formatted_pubs.append(entry)
   364→        return formatted_pubs
   365→    
   366→    def render_html_preview(
   367→        self,
   368→        job_analysis: Dict,
   369→        customizations: Dict,
   370→        approved_rewrites: Optional[List[Dict]] = None,
   371→        spell_audit: Optional[List[Dict]] = None,
   372→        max_skills: Optional[int] = None,
   373→        template_variant: str = 'standard',
   374→    ) -> str:
   375→        """Render CV as HTML for preview without generating PDF or DOCX.
   376→
   377→        Called by the staged generation workflow (GAP-20) to produce the
   378→        preview artifact that the layout-review loop works against.  Does not
   379→        write any files; returns the raw HTML string.
   380→
   381→        Parameters mirror ``generate_cv`` but only the HTML rendering path is
   382→        executed, so this is significantly faster than a full generation run.
   383→        """
   384→        selected_content = self.build_render_ready_content(
   385→            job_analysis,
   386→            customizations,
   387→            approved_rewrites=approved_rewrites,
   388→            spell_audit=spell_audit,
   389→            max_skills=max_skills,
   390→        )
   391→        cv_data = self._prepare_cv_data_for_template(
   392→            selected_content, job_analysis, template_variant
   393→        )
   394→        cv_data['achievements'] = selected_content.get('achievements', [])
   395→        cv_data['json_ld_str'] = self._build_json_ld(cv_data, job_analysis)
   396→
   397→        template_dir = Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / 'templates'
   398→        template_file = template_dir / 'cv-template.html'
   399→        if not template_file.exists():
   400→            raise FileNotFoundError(f"HTML template not found: {template_file}")
   401→
   402→        from .template_renderer import load_template, render_template  # noqa: PLC0415
   403→        template = load_template(str(template_file))
   404→        return render_template(template, cv_data)
   405→
   406→    def generate_final_from_confirmed_html(
   407→        self,
   408→        confirmed_html: str,
   409→        output_dir: Path,
   410→        filename_base: str = "CV_final",
   411→    ) -> Dict:
   412→        """Write confirmed HTML to disk and regenerate the human-readable PDF.
   413→
   414→        Called by ``POST /api/cv/generate-final`` after layout confirmation.
   415→        The confirmed HTML (which may have had layout instructions applied) is
   416→        written to ``output_dir/{filename_base}.html`` and converted to a PDF
   417→        via WeasyPrint.  ATS DOCX is not regenerated here because it is derived
   418→        from structured data, not from HTML layout.
   419→
   420→        Returns:
   421→            dict with keys ``html`` and ``pdf`` (absolute path strings).
   422→        """
   423→        output_dir = Path(output_dir)
   424→        output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   425→
   426→        html_path = output_dir / f"{filename_base}.html"
   427→        html_path.write_text(confirmed_html, encoding="utf-8")
   428→
   429→        pdf_path = output_dir / f"{filename_base}.pdf"
   430→        self._convert_html_to_pdf(html_path, pdf_path)
   431→
   432→        return {
   433→            "html": str(html_path),
   434→            "pdf":  str(pdf_path),
   435→        }
   436→
   437→    def _render_cv_html_pdf(
   438→        self,
   439→        cv_data: Dict,
   440→        output_dir: Path,
   441→        filename_base: str,
   442→        template_variant: str = 'standard'
   443→    ) -> Path:
   444→        """Render CV using the Jinja2 HTML template and convert to PDF.
   445→
   446→        Uses `templates/cv-template.html` with the `cv_data` dictionary
   447→        produced by ``_prepare_cv_data_for_template``. The rendered HTML is
   448→        written to `output_dir` and then converted to PDF via WeasyPrint.
   449→
   450→        Returns a 2-tuple ``(html_output, pdf_output)``.
   451→        """
   452→        
   453→        # Get templates directory and template file
   454→        template_dir = Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / 'templates'
```

```text
     1→# Gaps Analysis: Source-Verified UI Review Findings
     2→

     4→**Sources:**
     5→- `tasks/gaps.md` (prior baseline)

    14→This document tracks the gaps that still remain after reviewing the current code against the latest source-verified story reviews. Status values below reflect the current implementation state, not prior specification intent.
    15→
    16→**Severity scale:**
    17→`CRITICAL` - blocks a core workflow or acceptance path
    18→`HIGH` - major capability shortfall that should be fixed before the feature is considered complete
    19→`MEDIUM` - important omission or degraded UX that can slip one iteration if necessary
    20→`LOW` - polish or traceability issue that should be fixed, but is not the main blocker
    21→
    22→---
    23→
    24→## GAP-01: Master Data NL Update and Document Ingestion
    25→
    26→**Severity:** HIGH
    27→**Affected stories:** US-A10, US-A11
    28→**Status:** OPEN - verified 2026-03-19 11:36 ET; applicant review found the current Master CV UI only supports direct saves for summaries and achievements, with no natural-language update flow, document-ingestion review step, JSON diff preview, or per-change git commit.
    29→**Description:** US-A10 is still unimplemented in the reviewed code. The app does not let the user describe a change in plain language, upload an external CV/LinkedIn export for structured extraction, review a proposed JSON diff, and explicitly confirm before writing to `Master_CV_Data.json`.
    30→**Recommended resolution:** Add a dedicated master-data update flow that supports NL-to-structured proposals, document ingestion, explicit diff review, confirmation before write, full-file backup, and git commit on every confirmed update.
    31→
    32→## GAP-02: Iterative Refinement and Phase Re-Entry Completeness
    33→
    34→**Severity:** HIGH
    35→**Affected stories:** US-A6, US-A12, US-U1
    36→**Status:** PARTIAL - verified 2026-03-19 11:36 ET; applicant review confirmed `back_to_phase()` and `re_run_phase()` exist, but layout-only refinement is not routed into a working layout-review loop and per-cycle metadata refresh was not source-verified.
    37→**Description:** Targeted re-entry is no longer missing, but the workflow is still incomplete. Earlier-stage re-entry works for analysis/customization/rewrite paths, while layout-only refinement, changed-item highlighting, and archive/metadata refresh guarantees remain unresolved.
    38→**Recommended resolution:** Preserve the existing re-entry APIs, then add layout-only routing, changed-vs-unchanged review highlighting, and explicit archive/metadata update rules for every regeneration cycle.
    39→
    40→## GAP-03: Finalise and Archive Completion
    41→
    42→**Severity:** HIGH
    43→**Affected stories:** US-A9
    44→**Status:** PARTIAL - verified 2026-03-19 11:36 ET; applicant review confirmed finalise writes status/notes and creates a git commit, but Drive sync is still absent and the summary view does not show the requested keyword-match score.
    45→**Description:** The finalise flow is no longer blank, but it is not complete relative to the story. The archive metadata is updated and git commit automation exists, yet the Google Drive sync leg and the hiring-facing summary of match quality are still missing.
    46→**Recommended resolution:** Extend finalise to perform Drive sync with visible success/failure handling and add a post-generation summary card that surfaces ATS match score, missing hard requirements, and archived artefact status.
    47→
    48→## GAP-04: Post-Generation ATS Validation Coverage
    49→
    50→**Severity:** HIGH
    51→**Affected stories:** US-H6, US-A5c
    52→**Status:** PARTIAL - verified 2026-03-19 11:36 ET; HR/ATS review confirmed the ATS validation report exists, but it runs when the Download tab opens instead of automatically after generation, and several required checks remain incomplete or missing.
    53→**Description:** The validation framework is real and user-visible, but it does not yet satisfy the full acceptance surface. Missing or incomplete areas include keyword-density checking, PDF font embedding validation, full Heading 1 enforcement, complete JSON-LD required-field validation, and generation-time persistence into `metadata.json`.
    54→**Recommended resolution:** Trigger ATS validation automatically after final generation, expand the validator to cover the missing checks, and persist validation results at generation time rather than only during finalise.
    55→
    56→## GAP-05: CV Length Governance
    57→
    58→**Severity:** MEDIUM
    59→**Affected stories:** US-R2, US-M4, US-U6
    60→**Status:** PARTIAL - verified 2026-03-19 11:36 ET; resume and hiring-manager reviews found page-count warnings in the UI, but length control is still heuristic and not enforced across staged preview, layout review, and final generation.
    61→**Description:** The app now estimates and reports page length, so the gap is narrower than before. What remains missing is a consistent rule that carries length checks through preview, layout iteration, and final output, with clear thresholds and stage-appropriate warnings or blocks.
    62→**Recommended resolution:** Promote page-count thresholds into the staged generation contract, show warnings during preview and layout review, and ensure final ATS validation uses the same thresholds and messaging.
    63→
    64→## GAP-06: Rewrite Review Efficiency and Context Preservation
    65→
    66→**Severity:** MEDIUM
    67→**Affected stories:** US-A4, US-U5
    68→**Status:** PARTIAL - verified 2026-03-19 11:36 ET; applicant review passed the core rewrite card workflow, but UX review found edit mode hides the diff context and no sequential "approve and next" or compact review flow was source-verified.
    69→**Description:** The rewrite review surface is functional, but it still falls short of the more refined UX criteria. Users can review, edit, accept, and reject proposals, yet editing interrupts comparison context and larger rewrite batches lack an efficient rapid-review mode.
    70→**Recommended resolution:** Keep inline diff as the default, preserve before/after context while editing, and add a keyboard-friendly sequential review mode for larger rewrite sets.
    71→
    72→## GAP-07: Content Ordering Beyond Bullet Reordering
    73→
    74→**Severity:** MEDIUM
    75→**Affected stories:** US-A3, US-R2, US-U4
    76→**Status:** PARTIAL - verified 2026-03-19 11:36 ET; applicant review confirmed bullet reordering within an experience entry works, but no reviewed controls were found for reordering experiences, achievements, skills, or publications as full rows.
    77→**Description:** The story requirements extend beyond intra-job bullet order. The current UI lets the user reorder bullets inside a role, but not reorder the higher-level content blocks that determine what rises or falls in the CV.
    78→**Recommended resolution:** Add row-level reorder controls for each major review table, persist those order decisions in session state, and ensure final generation respects them across HTML, PDF, and ATS DOCX.
    79→
    80→## GAP-08: Spell and Grammar Resolution Path
    81→
    82→**Severity:** HIGH
    83→**Affected stories:** US-A4b, US-R7
    84→**Status:** PARTIAL - verified 2026-03-19 11:36 ET; applicant and resume reviews confirmed spell-check endpoints and audit persistence exist, but skill-name review, edit-in-place correction, blocking on unresolved flags, and write-back of accepted corrections into generated output are incomplete.
    85→**Description:** Spell check is implemented as a workflow step, but it does not yet behave like a reliable last-mile quality gate. The current flow can auto-ignore unresolved items, lacks a real edit path, does not emit `skill_name` review sections, and does not source-verify that accepted fixes alter the actual generated CV text.
    86→**Recommended resolution:** Add skill-name sections, force explicit resolution of flagged items, apply accepted corrections directly to the generated text span they govern, and keep the spell audit synchronized with the resulting output.
    87→
    88→## GAP-09: Action-Verb and Bullet Quality Enforcement
    89→
    90→**Severity:** MEDIUM
    91→**Affected stories:** US-M2, US-P4
    92→**Status:** PARTIAL - verified 2026-03-19 11:36 ET; hiring-manager and persuasion reviews confirmed strong-verb, passive-voice, and result-clause checks exist, but they remain advisory and do not enforce final-output compliance.
    93→**Description:** The system now detects several bullet-quality issues during rewrite review, which resolves the original "missing entirely" framing. The remaining gap is enforcement: weak bullets can still reach the final CV, and no reviewed minimum bullet-count, final line-length, or keep-together layout constraint closes the loop.
    94→**Recommended resolution:** Convert the highest-value bullet-quality checks into required review warnings or blocking checks before generation, and add final-output validation for bullet count, line length, and layout cohesion.
    95→
    96→## GAP-10: Keyword Normalization and Weighting
    97→
    98→**Severity:** MEDIUM
    99→**Affected stories:** US-R1, US-H4
   100→**Status:** PARTIAL - verified 2026-03-19 11:36 ET; resume review confirmed canonical synonym grouping exists, but deterministic title/lead-paragraph/repetition weighting and ATS-side variant normalization remain incomplete.
   101→**Description:** Keyword grouping is no longer the main problem. The unresolved piece is consistent weighting and visibility: the reviewed code does not clearly prove that job-title terms, repeated terms, and hyphen/slash variants are handled in a story-complete way across analysis and ATS validation.
   102→**Recommended resolution:** Formalize keyword weighting rules in code and spec, normalize slash/hyphen variants in ATS matching, and expose the resulting weighting model in the analysis and scoring UI.
   103→
   104→## GAP-11: Skills Canonicalization and Role-Aware Grouping
   105→
   106→**Severity:** MEDIUM
   107→**Affected stories:** US-R5, US-M3
   108→**Status:** PARTIAL - verified 2026-03-19 11:36 ET; deduplication and relevance ordering exist, but resume and hiring-manager reviews found no role-aware category ordering, no hard-vs-soft distinction, and no formal alias/write-back schema.
   109→**Description:** The app does a reasonable job of collapsing aliases into canonical skills, but the skills surface still lacks richer semantics. Categories are not clearly re-ranked by target-role relevance, and the reviewed pipeline still treats all skills as one general class for output and ATS reasoning.
   110→**Recommended resolution:** Add a richer skill schema with aliases, category intent, and hard/soft classification, then use it to drive role-aware grouping in both review tables and generated documents.
   111→
   112→## GAP-12: Candidate-to-Confirm Skill Evidence UX
   113→
   114→**Severity:** LOW
   115→**Affected stories:** US-R5, US-A4
   116→**Status:** PARTIAL - verified 2026-03-19 11:36 ET; reviewed UI surfaces AI-suggested skills, but source verification did not show a consistently strong evidence-specific `candidate_to_confirm` explanation tied to concrete experience evidence.
   117→**Description:** Candidate-to-confirm skills are not invisible anymore, but the current UX does not clearly explain why a given skill is weakly evidenced, what evidence exists, or what risk the user accepts by including it.
   118→**Recommended resolution:** Show the linked experience evidence directly in the skills review row, distinguish weak-evidence from simple new-skill suggestions, and align the badge language with the backend `candidate_to_confirm` flag.
   119→
   120→## GAP-13: Approved Skill Write-Back Workflow
   121→
   122→**Severity:** MEDIUM
   123→**Affected stories:** US-R5, US-A11
   124→**Status:** PARTIAL - verified 2026-03-19 11:36 ET; harvest apply can persist selected updates, but resume and applicant reviews showed approved extra skills remain "for this CV only" unless the user separately completes harvest, and evidence-linked dedupe rules remain unclear.
   125→**Description:** Skill persistence exists only as a later optional harvest step, which is weaker than the story intent. The path from approved extra skill to durable master-data update is indirect, easy to skip, and not clearly deduped against existing canonical skills.
   126→**Recommended resolution:** Make approved-skill persistence explicit in the review flow, carry supporting experience evidence into harvest proposals, and enforce canonical-dedupe rules before write-back.
   127→
   128→## GAP-14: Workflow Orientation and Stage Controls
   129→
   130→**Severity:** MEDIUM
   131→**Affected stories:** US-U1, US-A12
   132→**Status:** PARTIAL - verified 2026-03-19 11:36 ET; workflow chips, active/completed states, and guarded back-navigation exist, but restore messaging lacks richer context and not every completed stage exposes a visible re-run affordance.
   133→**Description:** The workflow indicator is no longer missing, but it is not yet complete as an orientation system. The stage chips do not fully cover the story's requirements for rerun discoverability, rich session restore context, and stage-specific user confidence.
   134→**Recommended resolution:** Add explicit rerun affordances for all eligible completed stages, expand restore messaging with last activity and preserved decisions, and align step labels with the actual stage names and actions.
   135→
   136→## GAP-15: Accessibility and Keyboard Coverage
   137→
   138→**Severity:** HIGH
   139→**Affected stories:** US-U7
   140→**Status:** PARTIAL - verified 2026-03-19 11:36 ET; modal focus management and validation wiring exist, but icon-only review controls still miss `aria-label` coverage and keyboard/reorder/focus behavior is inconsistent across primary controls.
   141→**Description:** Accessibility is no longer a blank slate. The reviewed app includes meaningful focus-trap and validation support, but several important controls still rely on weak semantics, incomplete labels, or uneven keyboard behavior.
   142→**Recommended resolution:** Add `aria-label` coverage to every icon-only action, normalize visible focus styles across all interactive elements, and ensure every reorder and review action is fully keyboard operable.
   143→
   144→## GAP-16: Broader UX and Information Architecture Gaps
   145→
   146→**Severity:** HIGH
   147→**Affected stories:** US-U2, US-U3, US-U4, US-U6, US-U8
   148→**Status:** PARTIAL - verified 2026-03-19 11:36 ET; the reviewed UI implements many core surfaces, but UX and heuristic reviews still found fragmented navigation, dense shell chrome, wall-of-questions clarifications, missing inline preview/versioning, and weak responsive behavior.
   149→**Description:** Earlier framing that these UX stories were "not implemented" is no longer accurate. The current state is instead a mixed implementation with major information-architecture and interaction-quality problems still open.
   150→**Recommended resolution:** Simplify the navigation model, chunk long clarification flows, add inline preview/version controls where output is reviewed, and redesign the shell for stronger 1280x800 and long-table behavior.
   151→
   152→## GAP-17: Persuasion Rule Enforcement
   153→
   154→**Severity:** MEDIUM
   155→**Affected stories:** US-P1, US-P2, US-P3, US-P4, US-P5, US-P6
   156→**Status:** PARTIAL - verified 2026-03-19 11:36 ET; persuasion review confirmed several checks exist for weak verbs, passive phrasing, CAR structure, and generic filler, but they are mostly advisory and do not cover narrative-arc, keyword-appendage, cover-letter persuasion, or cross-document register consistency.
   157→**Description:** Persuasion logic now exists in enough places that the old "artefacts do not exist anywhere" wording is obsolete. The current gap is that the rules are incomplete and often non-blocking, so the system can still produce rhetorically weak content even after warning about it.
   158→**Recommended resolution:** Expand persuasion validation to cover narrative arc, positive-sum framing, keyword stuffing, cover-letter openings/closings, and consistency between CV, cover letter, and screening responses.
   159→
   160→## GAP-18: Workflow Stage Re-Run Completeness
   161→
   162→**Severity:** HIGH
   163→**Affected stories:** US-A12, US-U1, US-A6
   164→**Status:** PARTIAL - verified 2026-03-19 11:36 ET; rerun endpoints exist and preserve downstream state, but applicant review found rerun affordances only for some stages, no clear clarification-amend path for analysis reruns, and no changed-item highlighting.
   165→**Description:** The core rerun mechanism exists, so the original gap is no longer unresolved at the foundation level. What remains is story-complete UX and rerun context management across all eligible stages.
   166→**Recommended resolution:** Expose rerun on every supported completed stage, allow clarification editing as part of analysis reruns, and compare old vs new results so only changed or new items require re-review.
   167→
   168→## GAP-19: Structured Master CV Editor
   169→
   170→**Severity:** HIGH
   171→**Affected stories:** US-A10, US-A11
   172→**Status:** OPEN - verified 2026-03-19 11:36 ET; the current Master CV surface still covers only summaries and selected achievements, and no reviewed pre-workflow editor supports structured maintenance of experiences, skills, education, publications, certifications, or personal info.
   173→**Description:** The specification work for a fuller editor may exist elsewhere, but the current product state is still an implementation gap. Users remain unable to manage most of `Master_CV_Data.json` through a structured in-app editor.
   174→**Recommended resolution:** Implement a dedicated Master CV mode with full structured editing, backups before save, import/export, preview, and write-back rules shared with harvest and other master-data update flows.
   175→
   176→## GAP-20: Staged HTML Preview, Layout Review, and Final Generation Workflow
   177→
   178→**Severity:** CRITICAL
   179→**Affected stories:** US-A5a, US-A5b, US-A5c, US-U6, US-U9
   180→**Status:** OPEN - discovered 2026-03-19 11:36 ET; applicant and UX reviews found no verified inline HTML preview pane, no complete frontend layout-instruction loop, and no distinct post-layout final-generation step derived from confirmed HTML.
   181→**Description:** The current workflow still behaves as a bundled generation path rather than the staged `HTML preview -> layout review -> final generation` flow required by the stories. Backend layout endpoints exist, but the reviewed frontend does not provide the story-complete preview, instruction, refresh, confirmation, and final-generation sequence.
   182→**Recommended resolution:** Split generation into explicit stages: generate HTML preview only, review/apply layout instructions against that HTML with visible preview refresh and history, then run final PDF/ATS DOCX generation from the confirmed HTML artifact.
   183→
   184→## GAP-21: ATS Match Score and Keyword Visibility
   185→
   186→**Severity:** HIGH
   187→**Affected stories:** US-H4, US-H7, US-A9
   188→**Status:** OPEN - discovered 2026-03-19 11:36 ET; HR/ATS and applicant reviews found no overall ATS match score, no live score updates as review decisions change, and no per-keyword matched/missing/bonus or section/match-type visibility.
   189→**Description:** The app surfaces keywords, missing requirements, and ATS validation warnings, but it does not provide the candidate with the unified ATS-fit scoring and keyword-level visibility the stories require. That makes it hard to judge whether customization choices improved or weakened ATS alignment.
   190→**Recommended resolution:** Add an ATS scoring model with hard-skill weighting, live recalculation during customization, per-keyword status labels, and persisted score details in generation metadata and final summaries.
   191→
   192→## GAP-22: ATS Document Structure and Skill-Type Semantics
   193→
   194→**Severity:** HIGH
   195→**Affected stories:** US-H1, US-H2, US-H3, US-H5, US-H8
   196→**Status:** OPEN - discovered 2026-03-19 11:36 ET; HR/ATS review found ATS DOCX heading levels and labels do not match the required standard, contact/date formatting is only partially normalized, and no hard-vs-soft skill classification or output separation exists.
   197→**Description:** The ATS output is close enough to validate, but not close enough to satisfy the stricter ATS-format stories. Structural semantics, heading conventions, contact normalization, employment-header formatting, and hard/soft skill typing all remain below the source-verified target.
   198→**Recommended resolution:** Normalize the ATS DOCX contract around approved heading labels and Heading 1 usage, enforce story-specific contact/date formatting rules, classify skills as hard vs soft, and represent that classification consistently in ATS DOCX, UI review, and JSON-LD.
   199→
   200→## GAP-23: Intake Metadata Confirmation and Clarification Defaults
```

# One-By-One Sequential Item Processor

Extract items from the current context and present them sequentially using intelligent prioritization. This system helps manage complex multi-item tasks by focusing on one item at a time with clear navigation.

## Process Overview

1. **Check for Existing Sessions**: Use `obo_list_sessions` for fast startup — do NOT read session files manually:

   **Step 1a — List sessions (fast path)**:
   - Call `obo_list_sessions(base_dir=<project_root>, status_filter="incomplete")`
   - Returns sessions where `status == "active"` AND `pending > 0` — these are resumable

   **Step 1b — Offer resume if incomplete sessions found**:
   - If resumable sessions exist, use `ask_questions` to present options:
     1. **Resume**: Continue from where the previous session left off
     2. **Merge**: Add new items to the previous session and resume
     3. **Defer**: Leave existing session for later and start a new session
     4. **Replace**: Mark existing session as completed and start fresh
     5. **Stop**: Stop /obo and let user handle manually
   - If no resumable sessions, proceed directly to item extraction

> **MCP unavailable?** Fall back to:
> `python3 ~/src/vscode-config/.github/skills/one-by-one/obo_helper.py <command> ...`
> Remove this note once `mcp.json` is updated to the GitHub URL (Phase C).

2. **Extract Items**: Analyze the current context to identify discrete items to process:
   - Todo list items
   - File contents or sections
   - Requirements or specifications
   - Code blocks or functions
   - Code review findings
   - Any enumerable tasks or components

3. **Calculate Priority Scores**: Evaluate each item using multiple factors:
   - **Urgency** (1-5): How time-sensitive is this item?
   - **Importance** (1-5): How critical is this to overall goals?
   - **Effort** (1-5): How much work is required? (lower effort = higher priority)
   - **Dependencies** (1-5): How many other items depend on this?
   - **Formula**: `priority_score = urgency + importance + (6 - effort) + dependencies`
   - Defaults when not specified: urgency=3, importance=3, effort=3, dependencies=1

4. **Persist Item List**: Create the session with `obo_create`:
   - Call `obo_create(base_dir=<project_root>, title=..., description=..., items=[...])`
   - Creates the session file and updates `index.json` atomically — no separate file writes needed
   - Items are passed as a list of dicts; priority scores are calculated automatically

5. **Analyze Dependencies and Order Items**: Identify dependencies between items and cascading impacts. Attempt to create a logical order based on dependencies (prerequisites first). If circular dependencies or conflicts prevent a single logical order, use `ask_questions` to alert the user and allow them to choose a different ordering approach (e.g., by priority score, alphabetical, or manual selection).

6. **Present Executive Summary**: Before sequential presentation, provide a high-level overview:
   - **Total Items**: Count of items to process
   - **Category Breakdown**: Group items by type/severity/area
   - **Priority Distribution**: How many high/medium/low priority items
   - **Estimated Effort**: Total effort required (sum of effort scores)
   - **Critical Items**: Count of urgent or blocking items
   - **Key Themes**: Common patterns or areas requiring attention
   - **Dependency Analysis**: Summary of dependencies and any ordering conflicts
   - **Recommended Approach**: Suggested order of operations

   **Example Summary**:
   ```
   ## Executive Summary

   **Total Items**: 12 findings
   **Categories**: 5 syntax errors, 4 anti-patterns, 3 optimizations
   **Priority Distribution**: 3 critical, 6 high, 3 medium
   **Estimated Effort**: 18 points (mix of quick fixes and moderate refactors)
   **Critical Items**: 2 data safety issues, 1 blocking syntax error
   **Key Themes**: Database connection management, parallel processing patterns
   **Dependency Analysis**: 3 items have dependencies; no conflicts detected
   **Recommendation**: Address 2 critical data safety issues first, then syntax errors, then optimizations
   ```

7. **Present Sequentially**: After summary, show the highest priority item with:
   - Clear focused context
   - Relevant background information
   - Specific actionable steps
   - Current progress indicator (e.g., "Item 3 of 12")

8. **Provide Navigation**: Use `ask_questions` tool to present clickable navigation buttons:
   - **Previous**: Return to previous item (if available)
   - **Approve Immediate**: Execute the described work for the current item immediately
   - **Approve Delayed**: Mark the current item as approved but delay execution until all items are reviewed
   - **Deny**: Deny current item and move to next
   - **Next**: Mark current item complete and move to next
   - **More Options**: Access additional navigation choices
   - **Elaborate**: Provide more detailed information about the current item
   - **List**: Show overview of all items with scores
   - **Summary**: Redisplay executive summary
   - **Reorder**: If ordering conflicts exist, allow user to choose different ordering approach
   - **Stop**: Stop /obo process and store current progress
   - **Help**: Display explanation of all navigation options
   - **Restore**: Reload original complete list from session file

---

## Session File Schema

Session files are plain JSON. Read and write them directly using the Read and Write tools.

**Path**: `.github/obo_sessions/session_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.json`

```json
{
  "session_file": "session_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.json",
  "created": "2026-03-10T14:30:00.000000",
  "title": "Human-readable session title",
  "description": "What this session is reviewing or working through",
  "status": "active",
  "items": [
    {
      "id": "phase-1",
      "title": "Short item title",
      "description": "Full description of the item and what needs to be done",
      "category": "General",
      "urgency": 5,
      "importance": 5,
      "effort": 2,
      "dependencies": 4,
      "priority_score": 18,
      "complexity": "S",
      "phase_deps": "none",
      "status": "pending",
      "resolution": ""
    }
  ]
}
```

**Item status values**: `pending` | `in_progress` | `completed` | `skipped`

**Session status values**: `active` | `completed`

**Optional item fields**: `complexity` (S/M/L), `phase_deps` (free text), `skip_reason` (populated on skip), `resolution` (populated on complete), `completed_at` (ISO timestamp on session close)

**Priority score formula**: `urgency + importance + (6 - effort) + dependencies`

---

## Session Operations (via MCP tools)

All session state changes go through MCP tools — do NOT use file tools (Read/Write/Bash)
for session JSON. The tools handle index.json updates automatically.

| Operation | MCP Tool Call |
|-----------|--------------|
| List sessions | `obo_list_sessions(base_dir, status_filter?)` |
| Create session | `obo_create(base_dir, title, description, items[])` |
| Session stats | `obo_session_status(session_file, base_dir?)` |
| Next item | `obo_next(session_file, base_dir?)` |
| List all items | `obo_list_items(session_file, base_dir?, status_filter?)` |
| Item detail | `obo_get_item(session_file, item_id, base_dir?)` |
| Mark complete | `obo_mark_complete(session_file, item_id, resolution, base_dir?)` |
| Skip item | `obo_mark_skip(session_file, item_id, base_dir?, reason?)` |
| Update field | `obo_update_field(session_file, item_id, field, value, base_dir?)` |

**`session_file`** accepts an absolute path or a bare filename (e.g. `session_20260314_120000.json`)
when `base_dir` is also provided.

**`obo_update_field`** auto-recalculates `priority_score` when `field` is `urgency`,
`importance`, `effort`, or `dependencies`.

### Mark item in-progress
Call `obo_update_field(session_file, item_id, "status", "in_progress", base_dir=...)`.

### Mark session complete
Call `obo_update_field(session_file, "session", "status", "completed", base_dir=...)`
— or update the session-level status directly; the tool handles `index.json`.

### Find next item to present
Call `obo_next(session_file, base_dir=...)` — returns `in_progress` items first
(highest priority_score), then highest-priority `pending`. Returns `{"message": "..."}` when done.

---

## Navigation Rules

- **Always use an interactive question tool for user input**: Any time user input or a decision is required, present structured choices — never wait for freeform text when options can be predefined
  - **VS Code Copilot Agent**: use `ask_questions`
  - **Claude Code**: use `AskUserQuestion` (exact equivalent)
- **Use the appropriate question tool for navigation** after presenting each item
  - **6-Option Limit**: both tools support maximum 6 options — use hierarchical [More Options] menus
  - **Primary Options** (main actions, shown first):
    1. Previous (return to previous item)
    2. Approve Immediate (execute work now)
    3. Approve Delayed (approve but delay execution)
    4. Deny (reject this item)
    5. Next (mark complete and continue)
    6. More Options (access secondary menu)
  - **Secondary Options** (meta-operations, shown after More Options):
    1. Elaborate (show more details)
    2. List (show all items with scores)
    3. Summary (redisplay executive summary)
    4. Reorder (change item ordering)
    5. Stop (save progress and exit)
    6. More Options (access tertiary menu if needed)
  - **Tertiary Options**:
    1. Help (explain all options)
    2. Save (flush all pending state changes to disk now)
    3. Restore (reload original list from session file)
    4. Back to Main Menu
  - **Option Bubbling**: Omit options not relevant to current context; allow others to move up
    - Omit "Previous" on first item → bubble up "Elaborate" or "Help"
    - Omit "Next" on last item if work incomplete → bubble up other options
    - Omit "Approve Immediate/Delayed" after already approved → bubble up
    - Omit "Reorder" if no ordering conflicts exist → bubble up
    - Omit "Restore" unless session was previously interrupted → bubble up
  - Use single-select mode; question header: "Navigate" or "Next Action"
- **When the user responds with freeform changes or additions** (rather than selecting a navigation option): Apply the requested changes to the relevant file(s), confirm what was changed, then re-present the **current item** with the navigation prompt — do NOT advance to the next item. Advancement only happens when the user explicitly selects Approve, Next, Deny, or Skip.
- **Write-on-change** — each MCP tool call writes to disk immediately (no batching needed):
  - Every `obo_mark_complete`, `obo_mark_skip`, and `obo_update_field` call persists to disk and updates `index.json`
  - No need to track unsaved state or show `(N unsaved)` — remove that indicator
  - "Save" in the tertiary menu can be omitted (or kept as a no-op confirmation)
- **Enable restoration**: When user selects Restore, Read the session file (last checkpoint) and re-display all items
- **Session cleanup**: When all items are finished, call `obo_update_field(session_file, "session", "status", "completed")` to mark the session complete. The tool updates `index.json` automatically.

**`session_file`** accepts an absolute path or a bare filename (e.g. `session_20260314_120000.json`)
when `base_dir` is also provided.

**`obo_update_field`** auto-recalculates `priority_score` when `field` is `urgency`,
`importance`, `effort`, or `dependencies`.

### Mark item in-progress
Call `obo_update_field(session_file, item_id, "status", "in_progress", base_dir=...)`.

### Mark session complete
Call `obo_update_field(session_file, "session", "status", "completed", base_dir=...)`
— or update the session-level status directly; the tool handles `index.json`.

### Find next item to present
Call `obo_next(session_file, base_dir=...)` — returns `in_progress` items first
(highest priority_score), then highest-priority `pending`. Returns `{"message": "..."}` when done.

---

## Navigation Rules

- **Always use an interactive question tool for user input**: Any time user input or a decision is required, present structured choices — never wait for freeform text when options can be predefined
  - **VS Code Copilot Agent**: use `ask_questions`
  - **Claude Code**: use `AskUserQuestion` (exact equivalent)
- **Use the appropriate question tool for navigation** after presenting each item
  - **6-Option Limit**: both tools support maximum 6 options — use hierarchical [More Options] menus
  - **Primary Options** (main actions, shown first):
    1. Previous (return to previous item)
    2. Approve Immediate (execute work now)
    3. Approve Delayed (approve but delay execution)
    4. Deny (reject this item)
    5. Next (mark complete and continue)
    6. More Options (access secondary menu)
  - **Secondary Options** (meta-operations, shown after More Options):
    1. Elaborate (show more details)
    2. List (show all items with scores)
    3. Summary (redisplay executive summary)
    4. Reorder (change item ordering)
    5. Stop (save progress and exit)
    6. More Options (access tertiary menu if needed)
  - **Tertiary Options**:
    1. Help (explain all options)
    2. Save (flush all pending state changes to disk now)
    3. Restore (reload original list from session file)
    4. Back to Main Menu
  - **Option Bubbling**: Omit options not relevant to current context; allow others to move up
    - Omit "Previous" on first item → bubble up "Elaborate" or "Help"
    - Omit "Next" on last item if work incomplete → bubble up other options
    - Omit "Approve Immediate/Delayed" after already approved → bubble up
    - Omit "Reorder" if no ordering conflicts exist → bubble up
    - Omit "Restore" unless session was previously interrupted → bubble up
  - Use single-select mode; question header: "Navigate" or "Next Action"
- **When the user responds with freeform changes or additions** (rather than selecting a navigation option): Apply the requested changes to the relevant file(s), confirm what was changed, then re-present the **current item** with the navigation prompt — do NOT advance to the next item. Advancement only happens when the user explicitly selects Approve, Next, Deny, or Skip.
- **Write-on-change** — each MCP tool call writes to disk immediately (no batching needed):
  - Every `obo_mark_complete`, `obo_mark_skip`, and `obo_update_field` call persists to disk and updates `index.json`
  - No need to track unsaved state or show `(N unsaved)` — remove that indicator
  - "Save" in the tertiary menu can be omitted (or kept as a no-op confirmation)
- **Enable restoration**: When user selects Restore, Read the session file (last checkpoint) and re-display all items
- **Session cleanup**: When all items are finished, call `obo_update_field(session_file, "session", "status", "completed")` to mark the session complete. The tool updates `index.json` automatically.
